topic: In China employee termination: scenario-led legal context guide for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination scenarios for foreign employers
related_keywords: China payroll offboarding for foreign companies

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Primary legal framework for termination and compensation exposure; essential for scenario interpretation.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Supports legal context for offboarding and termination-month statutory closure.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
sources.fit_note: Adds plain-language payroll tax context for non-local readers.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides multinational execution risk perspective to complement legal baseline.

consensus: Foreign employers need legal-context clarity first, then controlled execution.
Documentation and communication consistency strongly influence dispute risk.
Uncertain items should be separated and confirmed before payout.
uncertainties: City-level evidentiary standards and dispute practice may vary.
Some component treatment details require local confirmation.
English-language summaries may not include all local implementation nuance.
source_fit_assessment: Source set is sufficient and unchanged from prior run because it remains the strongest mix of official legal baseline + practical tax context + multinational execution analysis for foreign-reader scenario storytelling.
